actionable_feedback: Resolve metadata/label inconsistency: mark deterministic_precheck as artifact-contract-only (semantic_correctness_not_evaluated = true) and keep semantic_score only in the model/grader section, OR set deterministic_precheck.semantic_correctness_not_evaluated=false and document that semantic checks were performed. Ensure metadata fields are internally consistent.

Fix ambiguous wording in repair_decision.md regarding the sensitivity expert-review trigger: remove or rephrase the clause that reads 'so expert-review trigger not set correctly per spec' and clearly state whether the trigger was expected under the spec or intentionally not set.

Add one worked numeric example in the memo showing step-by-step +/-5% perturbation for a sampled record (include original value, perturbed value, recomputed observed_risk_score, recomputed margin, and final classification). Reference explicit workbook cells used in that worked example (e.g., 'Summary!B12' style).

Include at least one explicit end-to-end formula trace in the memo that cites workbook cell addresses for margin and classification logic so a reviewer can quickly verify calculations without loading the workbook. If possible attach an untruncated workbook preview or CSV extract of the Summary table.

Add a small assertion table or verification cells in the workbook that programmatically check: (a) margin = threshold - observed_risk_score for each row, (b) classification boundary handling (0% and +/-5%), and (c) recommended_combination_total_cost <= remediation_budget_usd. Cite those cells in the memo.

Shorten long narrative sections in the memo and add direct row/column cell citations for final-decision table entries to accelerate expert signoff (example: 'Summary!B5:B9 contains final classifications').
When re-uploading, ensure artifact previews are not truncated (or include full memo text in an additional plain-text artifact) so graders/verifiers can perform full-text checks.

verifier_issues.details: deterministic_precheck.metadata_json documents 'semantic_correctness_not_evaluated' while model/grader outputs include semantic_score = 1.0 and semantic grading reasoning. This is a metadata/label consistency issue.
verifier_issues.issue: Inconsistent semantic-evaluation flags vs semantic scoring
verifier_issues.recommendation: Clarify and align metadata flags: either keep deterministic_precheck as artifact-contract-only (semantic_not_evaluated=true) and restrict semantic_score to model grading outputs, or mark deterministic_precheck.semantic_correctness_not_evaluated=false and document the semantic checks performed.

verifier_issues.details: Some lines say the sensitivity check preserved classifications and therefore 'expert-review trigger not set', but another line reads 'not set correctly per spec' which can be misread as a logical error.
verifier_issues.issue: Ambiguous phrasing about sensitivity expert-review trigger
verifier_issues.recommendation: Edit the memo to remove ambiguous phrasing and explicitly state whether the sensitivity-trigger behavior matches the spec or whether a spec mismatch was observed.
